channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: China's Agricultural Revolution: Drones and IoT Transform Farming Efficiency and Quality
item.description: The article highlights how drones and Internet of Things (IoT) technology are revolutionising agriculture in China. Unmanned Autonomous Vehicles (UAVs) are being used extensively to optimise farming p...

item.title: Prime Volleyball League gains FIVB recognition for Season 5 starting November 30
item.description: The Prime Volleyball League (PVL) has received official recognition from the Fédération Internationale de Volleyball (FIVB), marking it as India's first division professional volleyball league. This r...

item.title: Reform UK's Internal Struggles as Nigel Farage's Resignation Sparks Political Turmoil
item.description: The article details the internal chaos within Reform UK following Nigel Farage's resignation, highlighting the aggressive tactics of Zia Yusuf as he attempts to salvage the party's reputation. Farage'...

item.title: Evaluating the Impact of Banning Cellphones in Schools on Student Performance
item.description: The article discusses the implementation of cellphone bans in Philippine schools, citing a DepEd order and Senate Bill No. 627. It highlights mixed evidence on the effectiveness of such bans, referenc...

item.title: NHL trade drama: Flyers' $18M offer for Leo Carlsson sparks debate over team's cap space
item.description: The article discusses the NHL's ongoing trade drama involving the Philadelphia Flyers' $18 million per-year offer for Anaheim Ducks' restricted free agent Leo Carlsson. Florida Panthers star Brady Tka...

item.title: Nigeria and Liberia Commit to Enhanced Cooperation and Economic Partnership
item.description: The Nigerian government has reaffirmed its commitment to strengthening bilateral relations with Liberia through enhanced political consultations, trade, investment, youth development, and regional coo...

item.title: Manhattan High-Rise Building Faces Structural Challenges After Column Collapse
item.description: A high-rise building in Manhattan, formerly the Pfizer headquarters, faced structural instability after steel columns buckled, prompting an emergency evacuation and ongoing stabilization efforts. The ...

item.title: Trump's ICE Nomination Sparks Concern Over Leadership Qualifications
item.description: President Donald Trump's recent nomination of Lance Schroyer to lead Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has drawn criticism from legal experts, who argue the choice reflects a shift toward loya...

item.title: Kitchener Community Grieves After Local Radio Station Closes
item.description: The Kitchener community is coming to terms with the closure of a long-standing local radio station, an event that has sparked widespread mourning and reflection. The article highlights how residents h...

item.title: European Airlines Halt Flights to Iran, Iraq, and Lebanon Due to Regional Security Concerns
item.description: European airlines have suspended flights to Iran, Iraq, and Lebanon due to escalating security concerns and regional tensions in the Middle East. The decision affects multiple carriers operating route...

item.title: NBI Testifies on VP Sara Duterte's Alleged Threats in Impeachment Trial
item.description: During the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Senior Agent John Mark Calilung testified that her alleged statements about instructing someone to k...

item.title: UK Considers Deporting Rochdale Grooming Gang Leader Amid Legal Challenges
item.description: The Home Secretary is set to amend the 1971 Immigration Act to enable the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, the former ringleader of a Rochdale grooming gang. Ahmed, 73, was jailed for 22 years in 2012 for...

item.title: SAD Plans to Screen Historical Film 'Satluj' Across Punjab to Highlight Militancy Era
item.description: The Shiromani Akali Dal (SAD) has announced plans to screen the film 'Satluj', previously titled 'Punjab ’95', across Punjab after its removal from an OTT platform. The movie details the life of activ...

item.title: Typhoon Inday Downgraded to Typhoon; PAGASA Updates on Path and Impact
item.description: Super Typhoon Inday, which entered the Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR) earlier this week, has been downgraded to a typhoon by the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services ...

item.title: Vintage Portraits of Pier Angeli on the Set of 'Somebody Up There Likes Me' (1956)
item.description: This article explores vintage photographs capturing Italian actress Pier Angeli during the filming of the 1956 boxing drama 'Somebody Up There Likes Me.' The images highlight her delicate beauty and q...

item.title: Top 10 F1 Drivers of the 2000s: A Decade of Dominance and Innovation
item.description: The article ranks the top 10 Formula 1 drivers of the 2000s, highlighting their achievements, impact, and contributions to the sport. It begins by noting the era's transition from V10 engines to newer...

item.title: Teenagers Arrested After Police Find $11K in Counterfeit Cash at Abbotsford Construction Site
item.description: A pair of teenagers were arrested after authorities discovered $11,000 in counterfeit currency at a construction site in Abbotsford. The incident occurred when police responded to the location and fou...

item.title: Trump Discusses Security Precautions and Iranian Threats During Air Force One Press Briefing
item.description: President Donald Trump addressed reporters aboard Air Force One while returning to Washington after the NATO summit in Turkey, responding to questions about unusual security measures during the flight...

item.title: Shift in AI Focus: From Model Development to Industrial Application
item.description: The 2026 AI Engineer World's Fair, held in San Francisco from July 1-4, marked a significant shift in the global AI industry's priorities. While previous editions focused on developing ever-larger mod...

item.title: SM Seaside Cebu Arena to Host 2027 FIBA Women's Asia Cup
item.description: The SM Seaside Cebu Arena has been officially announced as the venue for the 2027 FIBA Women's Asia Cup, a major international basketball tournament set to showcase the Philippines' capabilities in ho...
